revision.comment: [[石崎奉燈祭]]より一部転載
revision.text:
{{画像提供依頼|date=2011年12月|祭りの様子|cat=七尾市}}
'''向田の火祭り'''（むこうだのひまつり）は、[[石川県]][[七尾市]][[能登島]]向田町の伊夜比咩（いやひめ）神社の[[火祭り]]及び[[能登キリコ祭り|キリコ祭り]]である。毎年[[7月]]の最終土曜日に開催される。[[日本三大一覧#祭り|日本三大火祭り]]のひとつ。石川県指定[[無形民俗文化財]]。
== 概要 == 午後8時ころに御神燈を載せた[[神輿]]と供奉する大小7基の奉燈（キリコ）が伊夜比咩神社を出発、目的地の崎山広場には高さ約30mの柱松明（[[たいまつ]]）が立てられ、その周りを7周後、神輿の御神燈より地元住民と一般の観光客が持つ藁松明に移しグルグルと回しながら柱松明の周りを回る。笛の合図で一斉に柱松明に投げ込むと夜空を明るく照らす大きな炎となり、やがて大きく傾き崩れ落ちる。この崩れ落ちる方角により海側だと「豊漁」、山側だと「豊作」とされる。
[[1987年]]（[[昭和]]62年）1月14日に石川県の無形民俗文化財に指定、[[1997年]]（[[平成]]9年）12月4日に能登一円のキリコ祭りが、国の「[[記録作成等の措置を講ずべき無形の民俗文化財]]」に指定されているが、その中の1つである。また[[2015年]]（平成27年）4月24日には、「灯り舞う半島 能登 〜熱狂のキリコ祭り〜」の中の1つとして[[日本遺産]]に指定された。
== その他 == 柱松明に点火する藁松明は、一般観光客も希望者は自由に参加できる。
== 関連項目 == * [[火祭り]] * [[能登キリコ祭り]] * [[七尾市]] * [[能登島]] * [[日本三大一覧]] 「[[日本三大一覧#祭り|日本三大火祭り]]」 * [[記録作成等の措置を講ずべき無形の民俗文化財]] * [[日本遺産]]
== 参考文献 == * 『日本遺産 能登のキリコ祭り』（北國総合研究所 編・[[北國新聞社]]）[[2016年]]（平成28年）6月20日発行 ISBN 978-4-8330-2068-8
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.notojima.org/kouda 能登島観光協会オフィシャルサイト] * [http://www.nanaoh.net/p134.html 七尾市観光協会公式サイト] * [http://kimassi.net/notomaturi/himaturi.html きっまし金沢]
{{event-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:むこうたのひまつり}} [[Category:石川県の祭り]] [[Category:能登島]] [[Category:七尾市]]

{{画像提供依頼|date=2011年12月|祭りの様子|cat=七尾市}}

== 関連項目 == * [[火祭り]] * [[能登キリコ祭り]] * [[七尾市]] * [[能登島]] * [[日本三大一覧]] 「[[日本三大一覧#祭り|日本三大火祭り]]」 * [[記録作成等の措置を講ずべき無形の民俗文化財]] * [[日本遺産]]
== 参考文献 == * 『日本遺産 能登のキリコ祭り』（北國総合研究所 編・[[北國新聞社]]）[[2016年]]（平成28年）6月20日発行 ISBN 978-4-8330-2068-8
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.notojima.org/kouda 能登島観光協会オフィシャルサイト] * [http://www.nanaoh.net/p134.html 七尾市観光協会公式サイト] * [http://kimassi.net/notomaturi/himaturi.html きっまし金沢]
{{event-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:むこうたのひまつり}} [[Category:石川県の祭り]] [[Category:七尾市]]

== その他 == 藁松明を持ち柱松明へ点火することは、一般観光客も希望者は自由に参加できる。
== 関連項目 == * [[火祭り]] * [[能登キリコ祭り]] * [[七尾市]] * [[能登島]] * [[日本三大一覧]] 「[[日本三大一覧#祭り|日本三大火祭り]]」 * [[日本の祭り一覧]] * [[記録作成等の措置を講ずべき無形の民俗文化財]] * [[日本遺産]]
== 参考文献 == * 『日本遺産 能登のキリコ祭り』（北國総合研究所 編・[[北國新聞社]]）[[2016年]]（平成28年）6月20日発行 ISBN 978-4-8330-2068-8
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.hot-ishikawa.jp/kiriko/jp/index.php 能登のキリコ祭り] * [http://www.notojima.org/kouda 能登島観光協会オフィシャルサイト] * [http://www.nanaoh.net/p134.html 七尾市観光協会公式サイト] * [http://kimassi.net/notomaturi/himaturi.html きっまし金沢]

{{画像提供依頼|date=2011年12月|祭りの様子|cat=七尾市}}
'''向田の火祭り'''（こうだのひまつり）は、[[石川県]][[七尾市]][[能登島]]向田町の伊夜比咩（いやひめ）神社の[[火祭り]]及び[[能登キリコ祭り|キリコ祭り]]である。毎年[[7月]]の最終土曜日に開催される。[[日本三大一覧#祭り|日本三大火祭り]]のひとつ。石川県指定[[無形民俗文化財]]<ref>[http://www.pref.ishikawa.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunkazai/minzoku/k2-3.html 能登島向田の火祭（石川県公式サイト）]（2018年12月5日）</ref>。
== 概要 == 午後8時ころに御神燈を載せた[[神輿]]と供奉する大小7基の奉燈（キリコ）が伊夜比咩神社を出発、目的地の崎山広場には高さ約30mの柱松明（[[たいまつ]]）が立てられ、その周りを7周後、神輿の御神燈より地元住民と一般の観光客が持つ藁松明に移しグルグルと回しながら柱松明の周りを回る。笛の合図で一斉に柱松明に投げ込むと夜空を明るく照らす大きな炎となり、やがて大きく傾き崩れ落ちる。この崩れ落ちる方角により海側だと「豊漁」、山側だと「豊作」とされる。
[[1987年]]（[[昭和]]62年）1月14日に石川県の無形民俗文化財に指定、[[1997年]]（[[平成]]9年）12月4日に能登一円のキリコ祭りが、国の「[[記録作成等の措置を講ずべき無形の民俗文化財]]」に選択されているが<ref>[http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/161401 文化遺産オンライン（文化庁）]（2018年12月5日）</ref>、その中の1つである。また[[2015年]]（平成27年）4月24日には、「灯り舞う半島 能登 〜熱狂のキリコ祭り〜」の中の1つとして[[日本遺産]]に認定された<ref>[http://www.bunka.go.jp/seisaku/bunkazai/nihon_isan/ichiran.html これまでに認定された「日本遺産」一覧（文化庁）]（2018年12月5日）</ref>。

[[1987年]]（[[昭和]]62年）1月14日に石川県の無形民俗文化財に指定、[[1997年]]（[[平成]]9年）12月4日に能登一円のキリコ祭りが、国の「[[記録作成等の措置を講ずべき無形の民俗文化財]]」に選択されているが<ref>[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/161401 文化遺産オンライン（文化庁）]（2018年12月5日）</ref>、その中の1つである。また[[2015年]]（平成27年）4月24日には、「灯り舞う半島 能登 〜熱狂のキリコ祭り〜」の中の1つとして[[日本遺産]]に認定された<ref>[http://www.bunka.go.jp/seisaku/bunkazai/nihon_isan/ichiran.html これまでに認定された「日本遺産」一覧（文化庁）]（2018年12月5日）</ref>。
== その他 == 藁松明を持ち柱松明へ点火することは、一般観光客も希望者は自由に参加できる。
== 向田の火祭りを紹介する施設 == * 和倉温泉お祭り会館 – 七尾市[[和倉町]]（[[和倉温泉]]街）にある、向田の火祭りを含む、七尾市の代表的な4つの祭りの山車等を展示し紹介する施設（[[山車会館]]）。 {{Main|和倉温泉お祭り会館}}
==脚注== {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * 『日本遺産 能登のキリコ祭り』（北國総合研究所 編・[[北國新聞社]]）[[2016年]]（平成28年）6月20日発行 ISBN 978-4-8330-2068-8
== 関連項目 == * [[火祭り]] * [[能登キリコ祭り]] * [[七尾市]] * [[能登島]] * [[日本三大一覧]] 「[[日本三大一覧#祭り|日本三大火祭り]]」 * [[日本の祭り一覧]] * [[記録作成等の措置を講ずべき無形の民俗文化財]] * [[日本遺産]] * [[和倉温泉お祭り会館]]
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.hot-ishikawa.jp/kiriko/jp/index.php 能登のキリコ祭り] * [http://www.notojima.org/kouda 能登島観光協会オフィシャルサイト] * [http://www.nanaoh.net/p134.html 七尾市観光協会公式サイト] * [http://kimassi.net/notomaturi/himaturi.html きっまし金沢]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:向田の火祭り]]
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11416021]]
Wikidata name: Kōda no Himatsuri
[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Image requested|date=December 2011|images of the festival|cat=Nanao City}}

'''Kōda no Himatsuri''' (向田の火祭り) is a [[:en:Festival of fire|Fire festival]] and {{ill|Noto Kiriko Festivals|lt=Noto Kiriko Festivals|lt=Kiriko Festival|ja|能登キリコ祭り|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiriko Festival|qq=}} held at {{ill|Iyahime Shrine|ja|伊夜比咩神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyahime Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Kōda Town|ja|向田町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōda Town|qq=|1=Kōda Town|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11416027}}, [[:en:Notojima|Notojima]], [[:en:Nanao, Ishikawa|Nanao, Ishikawa]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]]. The festival takes place annually on the last Saturday of July and is considered one of the {{ill|Three Great Fire Festivals of Japan|ja|日本三大一覧#祭り|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Great Fire Festivals of Japan|qq=|1=Japan's Top 3|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10311536}}. It is designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Property|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 民俗文化財|1=Folk Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24895900}} of Ishikawa Prefecture.<ref>[http://www.pref.ishikawa.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunkazai/minzoku/k2-3.html 能登島向田の火祭（石川県公式サイト）]（Accessed December 5, 2018）</ref> Alongside the {{ill|Seihaku Festival|ja|青柏祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seihaku Festival|qq=}}, {{ill|Okuma Kabuto Festival|ja|お熊甲祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okuma Kabuto Festival|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ishizaki Hōtō Festival|ja|石崎奉燈祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishizaki Hōtō Festival|qq=}}, it is one of the Four Great Festivals of Nanao.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.nanao.lg.jp/kikaku-s/shise/sesaku/1sogokekaku/honpen/documents/kihonkeikaku_1_4_5.pdf|title=Basic Plan Chapter 1: Cultural Sector|publisher=Nanao City|accessdate=August 4, 2024|page=88}}</ref>{{Refnest|group="Note"|Some sources consider the Four Great Festivals of Nanao to be the {{ill|Seihaku Festival|ja|青柏祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seihaku Festival|qq=}}, {{ill|Ishizaki Hōtō Festival|ja|石崎奉燈祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishizaki Hōtō Festival|qq=}}, {{ill|Nanao Gion Festival|ja|七尾祇園祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanao Gion Festival|qq=|1=Nanao Gion Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353501}}, and {{ill|Nanao Port Festival|ja|七尾港まつり|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanao Port Festival|qq=|1=Nanao Port Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353495}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://yamaguchi-ev.com/ishikawa-event/1692/|title=Ishikawa Prefecture Events: May|publisher=Yamaguchi Inc.|accessdate=August 4, 2024}}</ref>}}.

== Overview ==
Around 8:00 PM, a [[:en:Mikoshi|mikoshi]] bearing a sacred lantern departs from Iyahime Shrine, accompanied by seven large and small {{ill|kiriko|ja|キリコ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kiriko|qq=|1=kiriko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11297682}} lantern floats. At Sakiyama Plaza, a towering 30-meter pillar torch ({{ill|Taimatsu|ja|松明|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taimatsu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to たいまつ|1=torch|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q327954}}) is erected. After circling it seven times, the sacred flame from the mikoshi is transferred to torches held by local residents and visitors, who spin them while circling the pillar torch. At a signal from a flute, the torches are thrown into the pillar torch, which bursts into bright flames. The direction in which the torch collapses predicts the year’s fortune: "abundant harvest" if it falls towards the mountain, and "plentiful catch" if it falls towards the sea.

The festival was designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Ishikawa Prefecture on {{ill|January 14, 1987|ja|昭和62nd year 1月14日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/January 14, 1987|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and included in the national designation of "Intangible Folk Cultural Properties requiring measures for documentation" on {{ill|December 4, 1997|ja|平成9th year 12月4日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 4, 1997|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as part of the Noto Kiriko Festivals.<ref>[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/161401 Cultural Heritage Online (Agency for Cultural Affairs)]（Accessed December 5, 2018）</ref> It was also recognized as part of the [[:en:Japan Heritage|Japan Heritage]] titled "The Light Dancing Peninsula, Noto: The Passion of the Kiriko Festivals" on {{ill|April 24, 2015|ja|平成27th year 4月24日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 24, 2015|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>[http://www.bunka.go.jp/seisaku/bunkazai/nihon_isan/ichiran.html List of Recognized Japan Heritage Sites (Agency for Cultural Affairs)]（Accessed December 5, 2018）</ref>.

== Facilities Introducing Kōda no Himatsuri ==
* {{ill|Wakura Onsen Festival Hall|ja|和倉温泉お祭り会館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakura Onsen Festival Hall|qq=}} – Located in {{ill|Wakura Town|ja|和倉町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakura Town|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 田鶴浜町|1=Tatsuruhama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7688334}}, [[:en:Wakura Onsen|Wakura Onsen]], this museum showcases the Kōda no Himatsuri along with the four major festivals of Nanao through displays of festival floats and exhibits. {{Main|Wakura Onsen Festival Hall}}

'''Kōda no Himatsuri''' (向田の火祭り) is a [[:en:Festival of fire|Fire festival]] and {{ill|Noto Kiriko Festivals|lt=Noto Kiriko Festivals|lt=Kiriko Festival|ja|能登キリコ祭り|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiriko Festival|qq=}} held at {{ill|Iyahime Shrine|ja|伊夜比咩神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyahime Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Kōda Town|ja|向田町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōda Town|qq=|1=Kōda Town|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11416027}}, [[:en:Notojima|Notojima]], [[:en:Nanao, Ishikawa|Nanao, Ishikawa]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]]. The festival takes place annually on the last Saturday of July and is considered one of the {{ill|Three Great Fire Festivals of Japan|ja|日本三大一覧#祭り|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Great Fire Festivals of Japan|qq=|1=Japan's Top 3|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10311536}}. It is designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Property|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 民俗文化財|1=Folk Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24895900}} of Ishikawa Prefecture.<ref>[http://www.pref.ishikawa.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunkazai/minzoku/k2-3.html 能登島向田の火祭（石川県公式サイト）]（Accessed December 5, 2018）</ref> Alongside the {{ill|Seihaku Festival|ja|青柏祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seihaku Festival|qq=}}, {{ill|Okuma Kabuto Festival|ja|お熊甲祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okuma Kabuto Festival|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ishizaki Hōtō Festival|ja|石崎奉燈祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishizaki Hōtō Festival|qq=}}, it is one of the Four Great Festivals of Nanao.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.nanao.lg.jp/kikaku-s/shise/sesaku/1sogokekaku/honpen/documents/kihonkeikaku_1_4_5.pdf|title=Basic Plan Chapter 1: Cultural Sector|publisher=Nanao City|accessdate=August 4, 2024|page=88}}</ref>{{Refnest|group="Note"|Some sources consider the Four Great Festivals of Nanao to be the {{ill|Seihaku Festival|ja|青柏祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seihaku Festival|qq=}}, {{ill|Ishizaki Hōtō Festival|ja|石崎奉燈祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishizaki Hōtō Festival|qq=}}, {{ill|Nanao Gion Festival|ja|七尾祇園祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanao Gion Festival|qq=|1=Nanao Gion Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353501}}, and {{ill|Nanao Port Festival|ja|七尾港まつり|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanao Port Festival|qq=|1=Nanao Port Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353495}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://yamaguchi-ev.com/ishikawa-event/1692/|title=Ishikawa Prefecture Events: May|publisher=Yamaguchi Inc.|accessdate=August 4, 2024}}</ref>}}.

== Overview ==
Around 8:00 PM, a [[:en:Mikoshi|mikoshi]] bearing a sacred lantern departs from Iyahime Shrine, accompanied by seven large and small {{ill|kiriko|ja|キリコ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kiriko|qq=|1=kiriko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11297682}} lantern floats. At Sakiyama Plaza, a towering 30-meter pillar torch ({{ill|Taimatsu|ja|松明|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taimatsu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to たいまつ|1=torch|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q327954}}) is erected. After circling it seven times, the sacred flame from the mikoshi is transferred to torches held by local residents and visitors, who spin them while circling the pillar torch. At a signal from a flute, the torches are thrown into the pillar torch, which bursts into bright flames. The direction in which the torch collapses predicts the year’s fortune: "abundant harvest" if it falls towards the mountain, and "plentiful catch" if it falls towards the sea.

The festival was designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Ishikawa Prefecture on {{ill|January 14, 1987|ja|昭和62nd year 1月14日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/January 14, 1987|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and included in the national designation of "Intangible Folk Cultural Properties requiring measures for documentation" on {{ill|December 4, 1997|ja|平成9th year 12月4日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 4, 1997|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as part of the Noto Kiriko Festivals.<ref>[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/161401 Cultural Heritage Online (Agency for Cultural Affairs)]（Accessed December 5, 2018）</ref> It was also recognized as part of the [[:en:Japan Heritage|Japan Heritage]] titled "The Light Dancing Peninsula, Noto: The Passion of the Kiriko Festivals" on {{ill|April 24, 2015|ja|平成27th year 4月24日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 24, 2015|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>[http://www.bunka.go.jp/seisaku/bunkazai/nihon_isan/ichiran.html List of Recognized Japan Heritage Sites (Agency for Cultural Affairs)]（Accessed December 5, 2018）</ref>.

== Facilities Introducing Kōda no Himatsuri ==
* {{ill|Wakura Onsen Festival Hall|ja|和倉温泉お祭り会館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakura Onsen Festival Hall|qq=}} – Located in {{ill|Wakura Town|ja|和倉町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakura Town|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 田鶴浜町|1=Tatsuruhama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7688334}}, [[:en:Wakura Onsen|Wakura Onsen]], this museum showcases the Kōda no Himatsuri along with the four major festivals of Nanao through displays of festival floats and exhibits. {{Main|Wakura Onsen Festival Hall}}

revision.comment: Bot: Add Wikidata no short description - based on Wikidata English short description
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:向田の火祭り]]
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11416021]]
Wikidata name: Kōda no Himatsuri
[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Image requested|date=December 2011|images of the festival|cat=Nanao City}}

'''Kōda no Himatsuri''' (向田の火祭り) is a [[:en:Festival of fire|Fire festival]] and {{ill|Noto Kiriko Festivals|lt=Noto Kiriko Festivals|lt=Kiriko Festival|ja|能登キリコ祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiriko Festival|qq=}} held at {{ill|Iyahime Shrine|ja|伊夜比咩神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyahime Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Kōda Town|ja|向田町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōda Town|1=Kōda Town|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11416027}}, [[:en:Notojima|Notojima]], [[:en:Nanao, Ishikawa|Nanao, Ishikawa]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]]. The festival takes place annually on the last Saturday of July and is considered one of the {{ill|Three Great Fire Festivals of Japan|ja|日本三大一覧#祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Great Fire Festivals of Japan|1=Japan's Top 3|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10311536}}. It is designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 民俗文化財|1=Folk Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24895900}} of Ishikawa Prefecture.<ref>[http://www.pref.ishikawa.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunkazai/minzoku/k2-3.html 能登島向田の火祭（石川県公式サイト）]（Accessed December 5, 2018）</ref> Alongside the {{ill|Seihaku Festival|ja|青柏祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seihaku Festival|qq=}}, {{ill|Okuma Kabuto Festival|ja|お熊甲祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okuma Kabuto Festival|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ishizaki Hōtō Festival|ja|石崎奉燈祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishizaki Hōtō Festival|qq=}}, it is one of the Four Great Festivals of Nanao.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.nanao.lg.jp/kikaku-s/shise/sesaku/1sogokekaku/honpen/documents/kihonkeikaku_1_4_5.pdf|title=Basic Plan Chapter 1: Cultural Sector|publisher=Nanao City|accessdate=August 4, 2024|page=88}}</ref>{{Refnest|group="Note"|Some sources consider the Four Great Festivals of Nanao to be the {{ill|Seihaku Festival|ja|青柏祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seihaku Festival|qq=}}, {{ill|Ishizaki Hōtō Festival|ja|石崎奉燈祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishizaki Hōtō Festival|qq=}}, {{ill|Nanao Gion Festival|ja|七尾祇園祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanao Gion Festival|1=Nanao Gion Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353501}}, and {{ill|Nanao Port Festival|ja|七尾港まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanao Port Festival|1=Nanao Port Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353495}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://yamaguchi-ev.com/ishikawa-event/1692/|title=Ishikawa Prefecture Events: May|publisher=Yamaguchi Inc.|accessdate=August 4, 2024}}</ref>}}.

== Overview ==
Around 8:00 PM, a [[:en:Mikoshi|mikoshi]] bearing a sacred lantern departs from Iyahime Shrine, accompanied by seven large and small {{ill|kiriko|ja|キリコ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kiriko|1=kiriko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11297682}} lantern floats. At Sakiyama Plaza, a towering 30-meter pillar torch ({{ill|Taimatsu|ja|松明|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taimatsu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to たいまつ|1=torch|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q327954}}) is erected. After circling it seven times, the sacred flame from the mikoshi is transferred to torches held by local residents and visitors, who spin them while circling the pillar torch. At a signal from a flute, the torches are thrown into the pillar torch, which bursts into bright flames. The direction in which the torch collapses predicts the year’s fortune: "abundant harvest" if it falls towards the mountain, and "plentiful catch" if it falls towards the sea.

The festival was designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Ishikawa Prefecture on {{ill|January 14, 1987|ja|昭和62nd year 1月14日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/January 14, 1987|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and included in the national designation of "Intangible Folk Cultural Properties requiring measures for documentation" on {{ill|December 4, 1997|ja|平成9th year 12月4日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 4, 1997|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as part of the Noto Kiriko Festivals.<ref>[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/161401 Cultural Heritage Online (Agency for Cultural Affairs)]（Accessed December 5, 2018）</ref> It was also recognized as part of the [[:en:Japan Heritage|Japan Heritage]] titled "The Light Dancing Peninsula, Noto: The Passion of the Kiriko Festivals" on {{ill|April 24, 2015|ja|平成27th year 4月24日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 24, 2015|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>[http://www.bunka.go.jp/seisaku/bunkazai/nihon_isan/ichiran.html List of Recognized Japan Heritage Sites (Agency for Cultural Affairs)]（Accessed December 5, 2018）</ref>.

== Facilities Introducing Kōda no Himatsuri ==
* {{ill|Wakura Onsen Festival Hall|ja|和倉温泉お祭り会館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakura Onsen Festival Hall|qq=}} – Located in {{ill|Wakura Town|ja|和倉町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakura Town|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 田鶴浜町|1=Tatsuruhama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7688334}}, [[:en:Wakura Onsen|Wakura Onsen]], this museum showcases the Kōda no Himatsuri along with the four major festivals of Nanao through displays of festival floats and exhibits. {{Main|Wakura Onsen Festival Hall}}

The festival was designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Ishikawa Prefecture on {{ill|January 14, 1987|ja|昭和62nd year 1月14日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/January 14, 1987|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and included in the national designation of "Intangible Folk Cultural Properties requiring measures for documentation" on {{ill|December 4, 1997|ja|平成9th year 12月4日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 4, 1997|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as part of the Noto Kiriko Festivals.<ref>[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/161401 Cultural Heritage Online (Agency for Cultural Affairs)]（Accessed December 5, 2018）</ref> It was also recognized as part of the [[:en:Japan Heritage|Japan Heritage]] titled "The Light Dancing Peninsula, Noto: The Passion of the Kiriko Festivals" on {{ill|April 24, 2015|ja|平成27th year 4月24日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 24, 2015|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>[http://www.bunka.go.jp/seisaku/bunkazai/nihon_isan/ichiran.html List of Recognized Japan Heritage Sites (Agency for Cultural Affairs)]（Accessed December 5, 2018）</ref>.

== Facilities Introducing Kōda no Himatsuri ==
* {{ill|Wakura Onsen Festival Hall|ja|和倉温泉お祭り会館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakura Onsen Festival Hall|qq=}} – Located in {{ill|Wakura Town|ja|和倉町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakura Town|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 田鶴浜町|1=Tatsuruhama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7688334}}, [[:en:Wakura Onsen|Wakura Onsen]], this museum showcases the Kōda no Himatsuri along with the four major festivals of Nanao through displays of festival floats and exhibits. {{Main|Wakura Onsen Festival Hall}}

[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Image requested|date=December 2011|images of the festival|cat=Nanao City}}

'''Kōda no Himatsuri''' (向田の火祭り) is a [[:en:Festival of fire|Fire festival]] and {{ill|Noto Kiriko Festivals|lt=Noto Kiriko Festivals|lt=Kiriko Festival|ja|能登キリコ祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiriko Festival|qq=}} held at {{ill|Iyahime Shrine|ja|伊夜比咩神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyahime Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Kōda Town|ja|向田町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōda Town|1=Kōda Town|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11416027}}, [[:en:Notojima|Notojima]], [[:en:Nanao, Ishikawa|Nanao, Ishikawa]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]]. The festival takes place annually on the last Saturday of July and is considered one of the {{ill|Three Great Fire Festivals of Japan|ja|日本三大一覧#祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Great Fire Festivals of Japan|1=Japan's Top 3|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10311536}}. It is designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 民俗文化財|1=Folk Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24895900}} of Ishikawa Prefecture.<ref>[http://www.pref.ishikawa.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunkazai/minzoku/k2-3.html 能登島向田の火祭（石川県公式サイト）]（Accessed December 5, 2018）</ref> Alongside the {{ill|Seihaku Festival|ja|青柏祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seihaku Festival|qq=}}, {{ill|Okuma Kabuto Festival|ja|お熊甲祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okuma Kabuto Festival|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ishizaki Hōtō Festival|ja|石崎奉燈祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishizaki Hōtō Festival|qq=}}, it is one of the Four Great Festivals of Nanao.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.nanao.lg.jp/kikaku-s/shise/sesaku/1sogokekaku/honpen/documents/kihonkeikaku_1_4_5.pdf|title=Basic Plan Chapter 1: Cultural Sector|publisher=Nanao City|accessdate=August 4, 2024|page=88}}</ref>{{Refnest|group="Note"|Some sources consider the Four Great Festivals of Nanao to be the {{ill|Seihaku Festival|ja|青柏祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seihaku Festival|qq=}}, {{ill|Ishizaki Hōtō Festival|ja|石崎奉燈祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishizaki Hōtō Festival|qq=}}, {{ill|Nanao Gion Festival|ja|七尾祇園祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanao Gion Festival|1=Nanao Gion Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353501}}, and {{ill|Nanao Port Festival|ja|七尾港まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanao Port Festival|1=Nanao Port Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353495}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://yamaguchi-ev.com/ishikawa-event/1692/|title=Ishikawa Prefecture Events: May|publisher=Yamaguchi Inc.|accessdate=August 4, 2024}}</ref>}}.

== Overview ==
Around 8:00 PM, a [[:en:Mikoshi|mikoshi]] bearing a sacred lantern departs from Iyahime Shrine, accompanied by seven large and small {{ill|kiriko|ja|キリコ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kiriko|1=kiriko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11297682}} lantern floats. At Sakiyama Plaza, a towering 30-meter pillar torch ({{ill|Taimatsu|ja|松明|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taimatsu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to たいまつ|1=torch|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q327954}}) is erected. After circling it seven times, the sacred flame from the mikoshi is transferred to torches held by local residents and visitors, who spin them while circling the pillar torch. At a signal from a flute, the torches are thrown into the pillar torch, which bursts into bright flames. The direction in which the torch collapses predicts the year’s fortune: "abundant harvest" if it falls towards the mountain, and "plentiful catch" if it falls towards the sea.

The festival was designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Ishikawa Prefecture on {{ill|January 14, 1987|ja|昭和62nd year 1月14日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/January 14, 1987|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and included in the national designation of "Intangible Folk Cultural Properties requiring measures for documentation" on {{ill|December 4, 1997|ja|平成9th year 12月4日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 4, 1997|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as part of the Noto Kiriko Festivals.<ref>[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/161401 Cultural Heritage Online (Agency for Cultural Affairs)]（Accessed December 5, 2018）</ref> It was also recognized as part of the [[:en:Japan Heritage|Japan Heritage]] titled "The Light Dancing Peninsula, Noto: The Passion of the Kiriko Festivals" on {{ill|April 24, 2015|ja|平成27th year 4月24日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 24, 2015|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>[http://www.bunka.go.jp/seisaku/bunkazai/nihon_isan/ichiran.html List of Recognized Japan Heritage Sites (Agency for Cultural Affairs)]（Accessed December 5, 2018）</ref>.

The festival takes place annually on the last Saturday of July and is considered one of the {{ill|Three Great Fire Festivals of Japan|ja|日本三大一覧#祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Great Fire Festivals of Japan|1=Japan's Top 3|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10311536}}. It is designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 民俗文化財|1=Folk Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24895900}} of Ishikawa Prefecture.<ref>[http://www.pref.ishikawa.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunkazai/minzoku/k2-3.html 能登島向田の火祭（石川県公式サイト）]（Accessed December 5, 2018）</ref> Alongside the {{ill|Seihaku Festival|ja|青柏祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seihaku Festival|qq=}}, {{ill|Okuma Kabuto Festival|ja|お熊甲祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okuma Kabuto Festival|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ishizaki Hōtō Festival|ja|石崎奉燈祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishizaki Hōtō Festival|qq=}}, it is one of the Four Great Festivals of Nanao.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.nanao.lg.jp/kikaku-s/shise/sesaku/1sogokekaku/honpen/documents/kihonkeikaku_1_4_5.pdf|title=Basic Plan Chapter 1: Cultural Sector|publisher=Nanao City|accessdate=August 4, 2024|page=88}}</ref>{{Refnest|group="Note"|Some sources consider the Four Great Festivals of Nanao to be the {{ill|Seihaku Festival|ja|青柏祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seihaku Festival|qq=}}, {{ill|Ishizaki Hōtō Festival|ja|石崎奉燈祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishizaki Hōtō Festival|qq=}}, {{ill|Nanao Gion Festival|ja|七尾祇園祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanao Gion Festival|1=Nanao Gion Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353501}}, and {{ill|Nanao Port Festival|ja|七尾港まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanao Port Festival|1=Nanao Port Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353495}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://yamaguchi-ev.com/ishikawa-event/1692/|title=Ishikawa Prefecture Events: May|publisher=Yamaguchi Inc.|accessdate=August 4, 2024}}</ref>}}.

== Overview ==
Around 8:00 PM, a [[:en:Mikoshi|mikoshi]] bearing a sacred lantern departs from Iyahime Shrine, accompanied by seven large and small {{ill|kiriko|ja|キリコ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kiriko|1=kiriko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11297682}} lantern floats. At Sakiyama Plaza, a towering 30-meter pillar torch ({{ill|Taimatsu|ja|松明|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taimatsu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to たいまつ|1=torch|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q327954}}) is erected. After circling it seven times, the sacred flame from the mikoshi is transferred to torches held by local residents and visitors, who spin them while circling the pillar torch. At a signal from a flute, the torches are thrown into the pillar torch, which bursts into bright flames. The direction in which the torch collapses predicts the year’s fortune: "abundant harvest" if it falls towards the mountain, and "plentiful catch" if it falls towards the sea.

The festival was designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Ishikawa Prefecture on {{ill|January 14, 1987|ja|昭和62nd year 1月14日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/January 14, 1987|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and included in the national designation of "Intangible Folk Cultural Properties requiring measures for documentation" on {{ill|December 4, 1997|ja|平成9th year 12月4日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 4, 1997|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as part of the Noto Kiriko Festivals.<ref>[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/161401 Cultural Heritage Online (Agency for Cultural Affairs)]（Accessed December 5, 2018）</ref> It was also recognized as part of the [[:en:Japan Heritage|Japan Heritage]] titled "The Light Dancing Peninsula, Noto: The Passion of the Kiriko Festivals" on {{ill|April 24, 2015|ja|平成27th year 4月24日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 24, 2015|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>[http://www.bunka.go.jp/seisaku/bunkazai/nihon_isan/ichiran.html List of Recognized Japan Heritage Sites (Agency for Cultural Affairs)]（Accessed December 5, 2018）</ref>.

== Facilities Introducing Kōda no Himatsuri ==
* {{ill|Wakura Onsen Festival Hall|ja|和倉温泉お祭り会館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakura Onsen Festival Hall|qq=}} – Located in {{ill|Wakura Town|ja|和倉町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakura Town|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 田鶴浜町|1=Tatsuruhama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7688334}}, [[:en:Wakura Onsen|Wakura Onsen]], this museum showcases the Kōda no Himatsuri along with the four major festivals of Nanao through displays of festival floats and exhibits. {{Main|Wakura Onsen Festival Hall}}

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11416021]]
Wikidata name: Kōda no Himatsuri
[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Image requested|date=December 2011|images of the festival|cat=Nanao City}}

'''Kōda no Himatsuri''' (向田の火祭り) is a [[:en:Festival of fire|Fire festival]] and {{ill|Noto Kiriko Festivals|lt=Noto Kiriko Festivals|lt=Kiriko Festival|ja|能登キリコ祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiriko Festival|qq=}} held at {{ill|Iyahime Shrine|ja|伊夜比咩神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyahime Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Kōda Town|ja|向田町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōda Town|1=Kōda Town|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11416027}}, [[:en:Notojima|Notojima]], [[:en:Nanao, Ishikawa|Nanao, Ishikawa]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]]. The festival takes place annually on the last Saturday of July and is considered one of the {{ill|Three Great Fire Festivals of Japan|ja|日本三大一覧#祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Great Fire Festivals of Japan|1=Japan's Top 3|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10311536}}. It is designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 民俗文化財|1=Folk Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24895900}} of Ishikawa Prefecture.<ref>[http://www.pref.ishikawa.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunkazai/minzoku/k2-3.html 能登島向田の火祭（石川県公式サイト）]（Accessed December 5, 2018）</ref> Alongside the {{ill|Seihaku Festival|ja|青柏祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seihaku Festival|qq=}}, {{ill|Okuma Kabuto Festival|ja|お熊甲祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okuma Kabuto Festival|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ishizaki Hōtō Festival|ja|石崎奉燈祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishizaki Hōtō Festival|qq=}}, it is one of the Four Great Festivals of Nanao.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.nanao.lg.jp/kikaku-s/shise/sesaku/1sogokekaku/honpen/documents/kihonkeikaku_1_4_5.pdf|title=Basic Plan Chapter 1: Cultural Sector|publisher=Nanao City|accessdate=August 4, 2024|page=88}}</ref>{{Refnest|group="Note"|Some sources consider the Four Great Festivals of Nanao to be the {{ill|Seihaku Festival|ja|青柏祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seihaku Festival|qq=}}, {{ill|Ishizaki Hōtō Festival|ja|石崎奉燈祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishizaki Hōtō Festival|qq=}}, {{ill|Nanao Gion Festival|ja|七尾祇園祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanao Gion Festival|1=Nanao Gion Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353501}}, and {{ill|Nanao Port Festival|ja|七尾港まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanao Port Festival|1=Nanao Port Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353495}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://yamaguchi-ev.com/ishikawa-event/1692/|title=Ishikawa Prefecture Events: May|publisher=Yamaguchi Inc.|accessdate=August 4, 2024}}</ref>}}.

== Overview ==
Around 8:00 PM, a [[:en:Mikoshi|mikoshi]] bearing a sacred lantern departs from Iyahime Shrine, accompanied by seven large and small {{ill|kiriko|ja|キリコ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kiriko|1=kiriko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11297682}} lantern floats. At Sakiyama Plaza, a towering 30-meter pillar torch ({{ill|Taimatsu|ja|松明|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taimatsu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to たいまつ|1=torch|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q327954}}) is erected. After circling it seven times, the sacred flame from the mikoshi is transferred to torches held by local residents and visitors, who spin them while circling the pillar torch. At a signal from a flute, the torches are thrown into the pillar torch, which bursts into bright flames. The direction in which the torch collapses predicts the year’s fortune: "abundant harvest" if it falls towards the mountain, and "plentiful catch" if it falls towards the sea.

The festival was designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Ishikawa Prefecture on {{ill|January 14, 1987|ja|昭和62nd year 1月14日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/January 14, 1987|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and included in the national designation of "Intangible Folk Cultural Properties requiring measures for documentation" on {{ill|December 4, 1997|ja|平成9th year 12月4日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 4, 1997|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as part of the Noto Kiriko Festivals.<ref>[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/161401 Cultural Heritage Online (Agency for Cultural Affairs)]（Accessed December 5, 2018）</ref> It was also recognized as part of the [[:en:Japan Heritage|Japan Heritage]] titled "The Light Dancing Peninsula, Noto: The Passion of the Kiriko Festivals" on {{ill|April 24, 2015|ja|平成27th year 4月24日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 24, 2015|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>[http://www.bunka.go.jp/seisaku/bunkazai/nihon_isan/ichiran.html List of Recognized Japan Heritage Sites (Agency for Cultural Affairs)]（Accessed December 5, 2018）</ref>.

== Facilities Introducing Kōda no Himatsuri ==
* {{ill|Wakura Onsen Festival Hall|ja|和倉温泉お祭り会館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakura Onsen Festival Hall|qq=}} – Located in {{ill|Wakura Town|ja|和倉町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakura Town|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 田鶴浜町|1=Tatsuruhama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7688334}}, [[:en:Wakura Onsen|Wakura Onsen]], this museum showcases the Kōda no Himatsuri along with the four major festivals of Nanao through displays of festival floats and exhibits. {{Main|Wakura Onsen Festival Hall}}

The festival was designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Ishikawa Prefecture on {{ill|January 14, 1987|ja|昭和62nd year 1月14日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/January 14, 1987|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and included in the national designation of "Intangible Folk Cultural Properties requiring measures for documentation" on {{ill|December 4, 1997|ja|平成9th year 12月4日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 4, 1997|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as part of the Noto Kiriko Festivals.<ref>[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/161401 Cultural Heritage Online (Agency for Cultural Affairs)]（Accessed December 5, 2018）</ref> It was also recognized as part of the [[:en:Japan Heritage|Japan Heritage]] titled "The Light Dancing Peninsula, Noto: The Passion of the Kiriko Festivals" on {{ill|April 24, 2015|ja|平成27th year 4月24日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 24, 2015|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>[http://www.bunka.go.jp/seisaku/bunkazai/nihon_isan/ichiran.html List of Recognized Japan Heritage Sites (Agency for Cultural Affairs)]（Accessed December 5, 2018）</ref>.

== Facilities Introducing Kōda no Himatsuri ==
* {{ill|Wakura Onsen Festival Hall|ja|和倉温泉お祭り会館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakura Onsen Festival Hall|qq=}} – Located in {{ill|Wakura Town|ja|和倉町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakura Town|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 田鶴浜町|1=Tatsuruhama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7688334}}, [[:en:Wakura Onsen|Wakura Onsen]], this museum showcases the Kōda no Himatsuri along with the four major festivals of Nanao through displays of festival floats and exhibits. {{Main|Wakura Onsen Festival Hall}}

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11416021]]
Wikidata name: Kōda no Himatsuri
[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Image requested|date=December 2011|images of the festival|cat=Nanao City}}

'''Kōda no Himatsuri''' (向田の火祭り) is a [[:en:Festival of fire|Fire festival]] and {{ill|Noto Kiriko Festivals|lt=Noto Kiriko Festivals|lt=Kiriko Festival|ja|能登キリコ祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiriko Festival|qq=}} held at {{ill|Iyahime Shrine|ja|伊夜比咩神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyahime Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Kōda Town|ja|向田町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōda Town|1=Kōda Town|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11416027}}, [[:en:Notojima|Notojima]], [[:en:Nanao, Ishikawa|Nanao, Ishikawa]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]]. The festival takes place annually on the last Saturday of July and is considered one of the {{ill|Three Great Fire Festivals of Japan|ja|日本三大一覧#祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Great Fire Festivals of Japan|1=Japan's Top 3|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10311536}}. It is designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 民俗文化財|1=Folk Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24895900}} of Ishikawa Prefecture.<ref>[http://www.pref.ishikawa.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunkazai/minzoku/k2-3.html 能登島向田の火祭（石川県公式サイト）]（Accessed December 5, 2018）</ref> Alongside the {{ill|Seihaku Festival|ja|青柏祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seihaku Festival|qq=}}, {{ill|Okuma Kabuto Festival|ja|お熊甲祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okuma Kabuto Festival|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ishizaki Hōtō Festival|ja|石崎奉燈祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishizaki Hōtō Festival|qq=}}, it is one of the Four Great Festivals of Nanao.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.nanao.lg.jp/kikaku-s/shise/sesaku/1sogokekaku/honpen/documents/kihonkeikaku_1_4_5.pdf|title=Basic Plan Chapter 1: Cultural Sector|publisher=Nanao City|accessdate=August 4, 2024|page=88}}</ref>{{Refnest|group="Note"|Some sources consider the Four Great Festivals of Nanao to be the {{ill|Seihaku Festival|ja|青柏祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seihaku Festival|qq=}}, {{ill|Ishizaki Hōtō Festival|ja|石崎奉燈祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishizaki Hōtō Festival|qq=}}, {{ill|Nanao Gion Festival|ja|七尾祇園祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanao Gion Festival|1=Nanao Gion Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353501}}, and {{ill|Nanao Port Festival|ja|七尾港まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanao Port Festival|1=Nanao Port Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353495}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://yamaguchi-ev.com/ishikawa-event/1692/|title=Ishikawa Prefecture Events: May|publisher=Yamaguchi Inc.|accessdate=August 4, 2024}}</ref>}}.

== Overview ==
Around 8:00 PM, a [[:en:Mikoshi|mikoshi]] bearing a sacred lantern departs from Iyahime Shrine, accompanied by seven large and small {{ill|kiriko|ja|キリコ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kiriko|1=kiriko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11297682}} lantern floats. At Sakiyama Plaza, a towering 30-meter pillar torch ({{ill|Taimatsu|ja|松明|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taimatsu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to たいまつ|1=torch|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q327954}}) is erected. After circling it seven times, the sacred flame from the mikoshi is transferred to torches held by local residents and visitors, who spin them while circling the pillar torch. At a signal from a flute, the torches are thrown into the pillar torch, which bursts into bright flames. The direction in which the torch collapses predicts the year’s fortune: "abundant harvest" if it falls towards the mountain, and "plentiful catch" if it falls towards the sea.

The festival was designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Ishikawa Prefecture on {{ill|January 14, 1987|ja|昭和62nd year 1月14日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/January 14, 1987|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and included in the national designation of "Intangible Folk Cultural Properties requiring measures for documentation" on {{ill|December 4, 1997|ja|平成9th year 12月4日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 4, 1997|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as part of the Noto Kiriko Festivals.<ref>[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/161401 Cultural Heritage Online (Agency for Cultural Affairs)]（Accessed December 5, 2018）</ref> It was also recognized as part of the [[:en:Japan Heritage|Japan Heritage]] titled "The Light Dancing Peninsula, Noto: The Passion of the Kiriko Festivals" on {{ill|April 24, 2015|ja|平成27th year 4月24日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 24, 2015|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>[http://www.bunka.go.jp/seisaku/bunkazai/nihon_isan/ichiran.html List of Recognized Japan Heritage Sites (Agency for Cultural Affairs)]（Accessed December 5, 2018）</ref>.

== Facilities Introducing Kōda no Himatsuri ==
* {{ill|Wakura Onsen Festival Hall|ja|和倉温泉お祭り会館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakura Onsen Festival Hall|qq=}} – Located in {{ill|Wakura Town|ja|和倉町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakura Town|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 田鶴浜町|1=Tatsuruhama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7688334}}, [[:en:Wakura Onsen|Wakura Onsen]], this museum showcases the Kōda no Himatsuri along with the four major festivals of Nanao through displays of festival floats and exhibits. {{Main|Wakura Onsen Festival Hall}}

'''Kōda no Himatsuri''' (向田の火祭り) is a {{ill|Festival of fire|en|Festival of fire|WD=Q3135724|lt=Fire festival}} and {{ill|Noto Kiriko Festivals|lt=Noto Kiriko Festivals|lt=Kiriko Festival|ja|能登キリコ祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiriko Festival|qq=}} held at {{ill|Iyahime Shrine|ja|伊夜比咩神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyahime Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Kōda Town|ja|向田町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōda Town|1=Kōda Town|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11416027}}, {{ill|Notojima|en|Notojima|WD=Q11612025|lt=Notojima}}, {{ill|Nanao, Ishikawa|en|Nanao, Ishikawa|WD=Q695892|lt=Nanao, Ishikawa}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|en|Ishikawa Prefecture|WD=Q131281|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture}}. The festival takes place annually on the last Saturday of July and is considered one of the {{ill|Three Great Fire Festivals of Japan|ja|日本三大一覧#祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Great Fire Festivals of Japan|1=Japan's Top 3|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10311536}}. It is designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 民俗文化財|1=Folk Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24895900}} of Ishikawa Prefecture.<ref>[http://www.pref.ishikawa.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunkazai/minzoku/k2-3.html 能登島向田の火祭（石川県公式サイト）]（Accessed December 5, 2018）</ref> Alongside the {{ill|Seihaku Festival|ja|青柏祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seihaku Festival|qq=}}, {{ill|Okuma Kabuto Festival|ja|お熊甲祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okuma Kabuto Festival|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ishizaki Hōtō Festival|ja|石崎奉燈祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishizaki Hōtō Festival|qq=}}, it is one of the Four Great Festivals of Nanao.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.nanao.lg.jp/kikaku-s/shise/sesaku/1sogokekaku/honpen/documents/kihonkeikaku_1_4_5.pdf|title=Basic Plan Chapter 1: Cultural Sector|publisher=Nanao City|accessdate=August 4, 2024|page=88}}</ref>{{Refnest|group="Note"|Some sources consider the Four Great Festivals of Nanao to be the {{ill|Seihaku Festival|ja|青柏祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seihaku Festival|qq=}}, {{ill|Ishizaki Hōtō Festival|ja|石崎奉燈祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishizaki Hōtō Festival|qq=}}, {{ill|Nanao Gion Festival|ja|七尾祇園祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanao Gion Festival|1=Nanao Gion Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353501}}, and {{ill|Nanao Port Festival|ja|七尾港まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanao Port Festival|1=Nanao Port Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353495}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://yamaguchi-ev.com/ishikawa-event/1692/|title=Ishikawa Prefecture Events: May|publisher=Yamaguchi Inc.|accessdate=August 4, 2024}}</ref>}}.

== Overview ==
Around 8:00 PM, a {{ill|Mikoshi|en|Mikoshi|WD=Q979940|lt=mikoshi}} bearing a sacred lantern departs from Iyahime Shrine, accompanied by seven large and small {{ill|kiriko|ja|キリコ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kiriko|1=kiriko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11297682}} lantern floats. At Sakiyama Plaza, a towering 30-meter pillar torch ({{ill|Taimatsu|ja|松明|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taimatsu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to たいまつ|1=torch|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q327954}}) is erected. After circling it seven times, the sacred flame from the mikoshi is transferred to torches held by local residents and visitors, who spin them while circling the pillar torch. At a signal from a flute, the torches are thrown into the pillar torch, which bursts into bright flames. The direction in which the torch collapses predicts the year’s fortune: "abundant harvest" if it falls towards the mountain, and "plentiful catch" if it falls towards the sea.

The festival was designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Ishikawa Prefecture on {{ill|January 14, 1987|ja|昭和62nd year 1月14日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/January 14, 1987|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and included in the national designation of "Intangible Folk Cultural Properties requiring measures for documentation" on {{ill|December 4, 1997|ja|平成9th year 12月4日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 4, 1997|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as part of the Noto Kiriko Festivals.<ref>[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/161401 Cultural Heritage Online (Agency for Cultural Affairs)]（Accessed December 5, 2018）</ref> It was also recognized as part of the {{ill|Japan Heritage|en|Japan Heritage|WD=Q18382798|lt=Japan Heritage}} titled "The Light Dancing Peninsula, Noto: The Passion of the Kiriko Festivals" on {{ill|April 24, 2015|ja|平成27th year 4月24日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 24, 2015|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>[http://www.bunka.go.jp/seisaku/bunkazai/nihon_isan/ichiran.html List of Recognized Japan Heritage Sites (Agency for Cultural Affairs)]（Accessed December 5, 2018）</ref>.

== Facilities Introducing Kōda no Himatsuri ==
* {{ill|Wakura Onsen Festival Hall|ja|和倉温泉お祭り会館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakura Onsen Festival Hall|qq=}} – Located in {{ill|Wakura Town|ja|和倉町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakura Town|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 田鶴浜町|1=Tatsuruhama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7688334}}, {{ill|Wakura Onsen|en|Wakura Onsen|WD=Q7961227|lt=Wakura Onsen}}, this museum showcases the Kōda no Himatsuri along with the four major festivals of Nanao through displays of festival floats and exhibits. {{Main|Wakura Onsen Festival Hall}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:向田の火祭り]]
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:向田の火祭り]]
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:向田の火祭り]]

{{Image requested|date=December 2011|images of the festival|cat=Nanao City}}

'''Kōda no Himatsuri''' (向田の火祭り) is a {{ill|Festival of fire|en|Festival of fire|WD=Q3135724|lt=Fire festival}} and {{ill|Noto Kiriko Festivals|lt=Noto Kiriko Festivals|lt=Kiriko Festival|ja|能登キリコ祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiriko Festival|qq=}} held at {{ill|Iyahime Shrine|ja|伊夜比咩神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyahime Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Kōda Town|ja|向田町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōda Town|1=Kōda Town|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11416027}}, {{ill|Notojima|en|Notojima|WD=Q11612025|lt=Notojima}}, {{ill|Nanao, Ishikawa|en|Nanao, Ishikawa|WD=Q695892|lt=Nanao, Ishikawa}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|en|Ishikawa Prefecture|WD=Q131281|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture}}. The festival takes place annually on the last Saturday of July and is considered one of the {{ill|Three Great Fire Festivals of Japan|ja|日本三大一覧#祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Great Fire Festivals of Japan|1=Japan's Top 3|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10311536}}. It is designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 民俗文化財|1=Folk Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24895900}} of Ishikawa Prefecture.<ref>[http://www.pref.ishikawa.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunkazai/minzoku/k2-3.html 能登島向田の火祭（石川県公式サイト）]（Accessed December 5, 2018）</ref> Alongside the {{ill|Seihaku Festival|ja|青柏祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seihaku Festival|qq=}}, {{ill|Okuma Kabuto Festival|ja|お熊甲祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okuma Kabuto Festival|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ishizaki Hōtō Festival|ja|石崎奉燈祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishizaki Hōtō Festival|qq=}}, it is one of the Four Great Festivals of Nanao.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.nanao.lg.jp/kikaku-s/shise/sesaku/1sogokekaku/honpen/documents/kihonkeikaku_1_4_5.pdf|title=Basic Plan Chapter 1: Cultural Sector|publisher=Nanao City|accessdate=August 4, 2024|page=88}}</ref>{{Refnest|group="Note"|Some sources consider the Four Great Festivals of Nanao to be the {{ill|Seihaku Festival|ja|青柏祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seihaku Festival|qq=}}, {{ill|Ishizaki Hōtō Festival|ja|石崎奉燈祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishizaki Hōtō Festival|qq=}}, {{ill|Nanao Gion Festival|ja|七尾祇園祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanao Gion Festival|1=Nanao Gion Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353501}}, and {{ill|Nanao Port Festival|ja|七尾港まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanao Port Festival|1=Nanao Port Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353495}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://yamaguchi-ev.com/ishikawa-event/1692/|title=Ishikawa Prefecture Events: May|publisher=Yamaguchi Inc.|accessdate=August 4, 2024}}</ref>}}.

== Overview ==
Around 8:00 PM, a {{ill|Mikoshi|en|Mikoshi|WD=Q979940|lt=mikoshi}} bearing a sacred lantern departs from Iyahime Shrine, accompanied by seven large and small {{ill|kiriko|ja|キリコ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kiriko|1=kiriko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11297682}} lantern floats. At Sakiyama Plaza, a towering 30-meter pillar torch ({{ill|Taimatsu|ja|松明|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taimatsu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to たいまつ|1=torch|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q327954}}) is erected. After circling it seven times, the sacred flame from the mikoshi is transferred to torches held by local residents and visitors, who spin them while circling the pillar torch. At a signal from a flute, the torches are thrown into the pillar torch, which bursts into bright flames. The direction in which the torch collapses predicts the year’s fortune: "abundant harvest" if it falls towards the mountain, and "plentiful catch" if it falls towards the sea.

The festival was designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Ishikawa Prefecture on {{ill|January 14, 1987|ja|昭和62nd year 1月14日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/January 14, 1987|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and included in the national designation of "Intangible Folk Cultural Properties requiring measures for documentation" on {{ill|December 4, 1997|ja|平成9th year 12月4日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 4, 1997|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as part of the Noto Kiriko Festivals.<ref>[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/161401 Cultural Heritage Online (Agency for Cultural Affairs)]（Accessed December 5, 2018）</ref> It was also recognized as part of the {{ill|Japan Heritage|en|Japan Heritage|WD=Q18382798|lt=Japan Heritage}} titled "The Light Dancing Peninsula, Noto: The Passion of the Kiriko Festivals" on {{ill|April 24, 2015|ja|平成27th year 4月24日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 24, 2015|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>[http://www.bunka.go.jp/seisaku/bunkazai/nihon_isan/ichiran.html List of Recognized Japan Heritage Sites (Agency for Cultural Affairs)]（Accessed December 5, 2018）</ref>.

== Facilities Introducing Kōda no Himatsuri ==
* {{ill|Wakura Onsen Festival Hall|ja|和倉温泉お祭り会館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakura Onsen Festival Hall|qq=}} – Located in {{ill|Wakura Town|ja|和倉町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakura Town|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 田鶴浜町|1=Tatsuruhama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7688334}}, {{ill|Wakura Onsen|en|Wakura Onsen|WD=Q7961227|lt=Wakura Onsen}}, this museum showcases the Kōda no Himatsuri along with the four major festivals of Nanao through displays of festival floats and exhibits. {{Main|Wakura Onsen Festival Hall}}

'''Kōda no Himatsuri''' (向田の火祭り) is a {{ill|Festival of fire|en|Festival of fire|WD=Q3135724|lt=Fire festival}} and {{ill|Noto Kiriko Festivals|lt=Noto Kiriko Festivals|lt=Kiriko Festival|ja|能登キリコ祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiriko Festival|qq=}} held at {{ill|Iyahime Shrine|ja|伊夜比咩神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyahime Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Kōda Town|ja|向田町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōda Town|1=Kōda Town|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11416027}}, {{ill|Notojima|en|Notojima|WD=Q11612025|lt=Notojima}}, {{ill|Nanao, Ishikawa|en|Nanao, Ishikawa|WD=Q695892|lt=Nanao, Ishikawa}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|en|Ishikawa Prefecture|WD=Q131281|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture}}. The festival takes place annually on the last Saturday of July and is considered one of the {{ill|Three Great Fire Festivals of Japan|ja|日本三大一覧#祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Great Fire Festivals of Japan|1=Japan's Top 3|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10311536}}. It is designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 民俗文化財|1=Folk Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24895900}} of Ishikawa Prefecture.<ref>[http://www.pref.ishikawa.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunkazai/minzoku/k2-3.html 能登島向田の火祭（石川県公式サイト）]（Accessed December 5, 2018）</ref> Alongside the {{ill|Seihaku Festival|ja|青柏祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seihaku Festival|qq=}}, {{ill|Okuma Kabuto Festival|ja|お熊甲祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okuma Kabuto Festival|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ishizaki Hōtō Festival|ja|石崎奉燈祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishizaki Hōtō Festival|qq=}}, it is one of the Four Great Festivals of Nanao.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.nanao.lg.jp/kikaku-s/shise/sesaku/1sogokekaku/honpen/documents/kihonkeikaku_1_4_5.pdf|title=Basic Plan Chapter 1: Cultural Sector|publisher=Nanao City|accessdate=August 4, 2024|page=88}}</ref>{{Refnest|group="Note"|Some sources consider the Four Great Festivals of Nanao to be the {{ill|Seihaku Festival|ja|青柏祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seihaku Festival|qq=}}, {{ill|Ishizaki Hōtō Festival|ja|石崎奉燈祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishizaki Hōtō Festival|qq=}}, {{ill|Nanao Gion Festival|ja|七尾祇園祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanao Gion Festival|1=Nanao Gion Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353501}}, and {{ill|Nanao Port Festival|ja|七尾港まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanao Port Festival|1=Nanao Port Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353495}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://yamaguchi-ev.com/ishikawa-event/1692/|title=Ishikawa Prefecture Events: May|publisher=Yamaguchi Inc.|accessdate=August 4, 2024}}</ref>}}.

== Overview ==
Around 8:00 PM, a {{ill|Mikoshi|en|Mikoshi|WD=Q979940|lt=mikoshi}} bearing a sacred lantern departs from Iyahime Shrine, accompanied by seven large and small {{ill|kiriko|ja|キリコ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kiriko|1=kiriko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11297682}} lantern floats. At Sakiyama Plaza, a towering 30-meter pillar torch ({{ill|Taimatsu|ja|松明|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taimatsu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to たいまつ|1=torch|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q327954}}) is erected. After circling it seven times, the sacred flame from the mikoshi is transferred to torches held by local residents and visitors, who spin them while circling the pillar torch. At a signal from a flute, the torches are thrown into the pillar torch, which bursts into bright flames. The direction in which the torch collapses predicts the year’s fortune: "abundant harvest" if it falls towards the mountain, and "plentiful catch" if it falls towards the sea.

The festival was designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Ishikawa Prefecture on {{ill|January 14, 1987|ja|昭和62nd year 1月14日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/January 14, 1987|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and included in the national designation of "Intangible Folk Cultural Properties requiring measures for documentation" on {{ill|December 4, 1997|ja|平成9th year 12月4日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 4, 1997|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as part of the Noto Kiriko Festivals.<ref>[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/161401 Cultural Heritage Online (Agency for Cultural Affairs)]（Accessed December 5, 2018）</ref> It was also recognized as part of the {{ill|Japan Heritage|en|Japan Heritage|WD=Q18382798|lt=Japan Heritage}} titled "The Light Dancing Peninsula, Noto: The Passion of the Kiriko Festivals" on {{ill|April 24, 2015|ja|平成27th year 4月24日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 24, 2015|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>[http://www.bunka.go.jp/seisaku/bunkazai/nihon_isan/ichiran.html List of Recognized Japan Heritage Sites (Agency for Cultural Affairs)]（Accessed December 5, 2018）</ref>.

== Facilities Introducing Kōda no Himatsuri ==
* {{ill|Wakura Onsen Festival Hall|ja|和倉温泉お祭り会館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakura Onsen Festival Hall|qq=}} – Located in {{ill|Wakura Town|ja|和倉町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakura Town|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 田鶴浜町|1=Tatsuruhama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7688334}}, {{ill|Wakura Onsen|en|Wakura Onsen|WD=Q7961227|lt=Wakura Onsen}}, this museum showcases the Kōda no Himatsuri along with the four major festivals of Nanao through displays of festival floats and exhibits. {{Main|Wakura Onsen Festival Hall}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:向田の火祭り]]
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:向田の火祭り]]
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:向田の火祭り]]

{{Image requested|date=December 2011|images of the festival|cat=Nanao City}}

'''Kōda no Himatsuri''' (向田の火祭り) is a {{ill|Festival of fire|en|Festival of fire|WD=Q3135724|lt=Fire festival|1=Fire Festival}} and {{ill|Noto Kiriko Festivals|lt=Noto Kiriko Festivals|lt=Kiriko Festival|ja|能登キリコ祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiriko Festival|qq=}} held at {{ill|Iyahime Shrine|ja|伊夜比咩神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyahime Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Kōda Town|ja|向田町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōda Town|1=Q11416027|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11416027}}, {{ill|Notojima|en|Notojima|WD=Q11612025|lt=Notojima|1=Notojima}}, {{ill|Nanao, Ishikawa|en|Nanao, Ishikawa|WD=Q695892|lt=Nanao, Ishikawa|1=Nanao, Ishikawa}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|en|Ishikawa Prefecture|WD=Q131281|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture|1=Ishikawa Prefecture}}. The festival takes place annually on the last Saturday of July and is considered one of the {{ill|Three Great Fire Festivals of Japan|ja|日本三大一覧#祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Great Fire Festivals of Japan|1=Japan's Top 3|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10311536}}. It is designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 民俗文化財|1=Folk Cultural Property|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24895900}} of Ishikawa Prefecture.<ref>[http://www.pref.ishikawa.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunkazai/minzoku/k2-3.html 能登島向田の火祭（石川県公式サイト）]（Accessed December 5, 2018）</ref> Alongside the {{ill|Seihaku Festival|ja|青柏祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seihaku Festival|qq=}}, {{ill|Okuma Kabuto Festival|ja|お熊甲祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okuma Kabuto Festival|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ishizaki Hōtō Festival|ja|石崎奉燈祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishizaki Hōtō Festival|qq=}}, it is one of the Four Great Festivals of Nanao.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.nanao.lg.jp/kikaku-s/shise/sesaku/1sogokekaku/honpen/documents/kihonkeikaku_1_4_5.pdf|title=Basic Plan Chapter 1: Cultural Sector|publisher=Nanao City|accessdate=August 4, 2024|page=88}}</ref>{{Refnest|group="Note"|Some sources consider the Four Great Festivals of Nanao to be the {{ill|Seihaku Festival|ja|青柏祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seihaku Festival|qq=}}, {{ill|Ishizaki Hōtō Festival|ja|石崎奉燈祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishizaki Hōtō Festival|qq=}}, {{ill|Nanao Gion Festival|ja|七尾祇園祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanao Gion Festival|1=Nanao Gion Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353501}}, and {{ill|Nanao Port Festival|ja|七尾港まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanao Port Festival|1=Nanao Port Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353495}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://yamaguchi-ev.com/ishikawa-event/1692/|title=Ishikawa Prefecture Events: May|publisher=Yamaguchi Inc.|accessdate=August 4, 2024}}</ref>}}.

== Overview ==
Around 8:00 PM, a {{ill|Mikoshi|en|Mikoshi|WD=Q979940|lt=mikoshi|1=Mikoshi}} bearing a sacred lantern departs from Iyahime Shrine, accompanied by seven large and small {{ill|kiriko|ja|キリコ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kiriko|1=Q11297682|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11297682}} lantern floats. At Sakiyama Plaza, a towering 30-meter pillar torch ({{ill|Taimatsu|ja|松明|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taimatsu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to たいまつ|1=Torch|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q327954}}) is erected. After circling it seven times, the sacred flame from the mikoshi is transferred to torches held by local residents and visitors, who spin them while circling the pillar torch. At a signal from a flute, the torches are thrown into the pillar torch, which bursts into bright flames. The direction in which the torch collapses predicts the year’s fortune: "abundant harvest" if it falls towards the mountain, and "plentiful catch" if it falls towards the sea.

The festival was designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Ishikawa Prefecture on {{ill|January 14, 1987|ja|昭和62nd year 1月14日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/January 14, 1987|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and included in the national designation of "Intangible Folk Cultural Properties requiring measures for documentation" on {{ill|December 4, 1997|ja|平成9th year 12月4日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 4, 1997|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as part of the Noto Kiriko Festivals.<ref>[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/161401 Cultural Heritage Online (Agency for Cultural Affairs)]（Accessed December 5, 2018）</ref> It was also recognized as part of the {{ill|Japan Heritage|en|Japan Heritage|WD=Q18382798|lt=Japan Heritage|1=Japan Heritage}} titled "The Light Dancing Peninsula, Noto: The Passion of the Kiriko Festivals" on {{ill|April 24, 2015|ja|平成27th year 4月24日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 24, 2015|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>[http://www.bunka.go.jp/seisaku/bunkazai/nihon_isan/ichiran.html List of Recognized Japan Heritage Sites (Agency for Cultural Affairs)]（Accessed December 5, 2018）</ref>.

== Facilities Introducing Kōda no Himatsuri ==
* {{ill|Wakura Onsen Festival Hall|ja|和倉温泉お祭り会館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakura Onsen Festival Hall|qq=}} – Located in {{ill|Wakura Town|ja|和倉町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakura Town|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 田鶴浜町|1=Tatsuruhama, Ishikawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7688334}}, {{ill|Wakura Onsen|en|Wakura Onsen|WD=Q7961227|lt=Wakura Onsen|1=Wakura Onsen}}, this museum showcases the Kōda no Himatsuri along with the four major festivals of Nanao through displays of festival floats and exhibits. {{Main|Wakura Onsen Festival Hall}}

'''Kōda no Himatsuri''' (向田の火祭り) is a {{ill|Festival of fire|en|Festival of fire|WD=Q3135724|lt=Fire festival|1=Fire Festival}} and {{ill|Noto Kiriko Festivals|lt=Noto Kiriko Festivals|lt=Kiriko Festival|ja|能登キリコ祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiriko Festival|qq=}} held at {{ill|Iyahime Shrine|ja|伊夜比咩神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyahime Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Kōda Town|ja|向田町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōda Town|1=Q11416027|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11416027}}, {{ill|Notojima|en|Notojima|WD=Q11612025|lt=Notojima|1=Notojima}}, {{ill|Nanao, Ishikawa|en|Nanao, Ishikawa|WD=Q695892|lt=Nanao, Ishikawa|1=Nanao, Ishikawa}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|en|Ishikawa Prefecture|WD=Q131281|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture|1=Ishikawa Prefecture}}. The festival takes place annually on the last Saturday of July and is considered one of the {{ill|Three Great Fire Festivals of Japan|ja|日本三大一覧#祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Great Fire Festivals of Japan|1=Japan's Top 3|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10311536}}. It is designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 民俗文化財|1=Folk Cultural Property|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24895900}} of Ishikawa Prefecture.<ref>[http://www.pref.ishikawa.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunkazai/minzoku/k2-3.html 能登島向田の火祭（石川県公式サイト）]（Accessed December 5, 2018）</ref> Alongside the {{ill|Seihaku Festival|ja|青柏祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seihaku Festival|qq=}}, {{ill|Okuma Kabuto Festival|ja|お熊甲祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okuma Kabuto Festival|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ishizaki Hōtō Festival|ja|石崎奉燈祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishizaki Hōtō Festival|qq=}}, it is one of the Four Great Festivals of Nanao.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.nanao.lg.jp/kikaku-s/shise/sesaku/1sogokekaku/honpen/documents/kihonkeikaku_1_4_5.pdf|title=Basic Plan Chapter 1: Cultural Sector|publisher=Nanao City|accessdate=August 4, 2024|page=88}}</ref>{{Refnest|group="Note"|Some sources consider the Four Great Festivals of Nanao to be the {{ill|Seihaku Festival|ja|青柏祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seihaku Festival|qq=}}, {{ill|Ishizaki Hōtō Festival|ja|石崎奉燈祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishizaki Hōtō Festival|qq=}}, {{ill|Nanao Gion Festival|ja|七尾祇園祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanao Gion Festival|1=Nanao Gion Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353501}}, and {{ill|Nanao Port Festival|ja|七尾港まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanao Port Festival|1=Nanao Port Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353495}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://yamaguchi-ev.com/ishikawa-event/1692/|title=Ishikawa Prefecture Events: May|publisher=Yamaguchi Inc.|accessdate=August 4, 2024}}</ref>}}.

== Overview ==
Around 8:00 PM, a {{ill|Mikoshi|en|Mikoshi|WD=Q979940|lt=mikoshi|1=Mikoshi}} bearing a sacred lantern departs from Iyahime Shrine, accompanied by seven large and small {{ill|kiriko|ja|キリコ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kiriko|1=Q11297682|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11297682}} lantern floats. At Sakiyama Plaza, a towering 30-meter pillar torch ({{ill|Taimatsu|ja|松明|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taimatsu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to たいまつ|1=Torch|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q327954}}) is erected. After circling it seven times, the sacred flame from the mikoshi is transferred to torches held by local residents and visitors, who spin them while circling the pillar torch. At a signal from a flute, the torches are thrown into the pillar torch, which bursts into bright flames. The direction in which the torch collapses predicts the year’s fortune: "abundant harvest" if it falls towards the mountain, and "plentiful catch" if it falls towards the sea.

The festival was designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Ishikawa Prefecture on {{ill|January 14, 1987|ja|昭和62nd year 1月14日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/January 14, 1987|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and included in the national designation of "Intangible Folk Cultural Properties requiring measures for documentation" on {{ill|December 4, 1997|ja|平成9th year 12月4日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 4, 1997|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as part of the Noto Kiriko Festivals.<ref>[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/161401 Cultural Heritage Online (Agency for Cultural Affairs)]（Accessed December 5, 2018）</ref> It was also recognized as part of the {{ill|Japan Heritage|en|Japan Heritage|WD=Q18382798|lt=Japan Heritage|1=Japan Heritage}} titled "The Light Dancing Peninsula, Noto: The Passion of the Kiriko Festivals" on {{ill|April 24, 2015|ja|平成27th year 4月24日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 24, 2015|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>[http://www.bunka.go.jp/seisaku/bunkazai/nihon_isan/ichiran.html List of Recognized Japan Heritage Sites (Agency for Cultural Affairs)]（Accessed December 5, 2018）</ref>.

== Facilities Introducing Kōda no Himatsuri ==
* {{ill|Wakura Onsen Festival Hall|ja|和倉温泉お祭り会館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakura Onsen Festival Hall|qq=}} – Located in {{ill|Wakura Town|ja|和倉町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakura Town|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 田鶴浜町|1=Tatsuruhama, Ishikawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7688334}}, {{ill|Wakura Onsen|en|Wakura Onsen|WD=Q7961227|lt=Wakura Onsen|1=Wakura Onsen}}, this museum showcases the Kōda no Himatsuri along with the four major festivals of Nanao through displays of festival floats and exhibits. {{Main|Wakura Onsen Festival Hall}}
